reasoning: As of late October 2025, Charles Leclerc holds a massive points lead over Lewis Hamilton (approximately 60 points, with Leclerc at roughly 191 points vs. Hamilton's 131 following the Mexican GP). With only 3-4 races remaining in the season, Hamilton would need to outscore Leclerc by an average of 15-20 points per race to overtake him. Given that Leclerc has dominated the head-to-head qualifying (12-5 or 13-4) and race results throughout the year, taking multiple podiums while Hamilton has struggled to adapt to the Ferrari SF-25, such a massive reversal is statistically highly improbable. Barring an incredibly unlikely streak of DNFs for Leclerc coupled with race wins for Hamilton, Leclerc is almost guaranteed to finish ahead of him in the 2025 standings.
